accessibility: Every mode and submit action has a visible Arabic label rather than an icon-only control.
Password fields use secure entry; phone and activation-code fields use appropriate mobile keyboards.
Loading, forbidden, unavailable, conflict, rate-limit, expired-session, and generic recovery states expose a title and description.
Submitting disables duplicate actions while preserving entered values.
Error presentation never depends on color alone and never exposes raw server details.
weakNetwork: Session restoration first validates the access token and then performs one governed refresh rotation.
A network failure preserves the sign-in form and maps to a retryable unavailable state.
Logout clears local credentials immediately and treats remote revocation as best-effort.
Activation and login controls prevent duplicate submission while a request is in flight.

performance.protectedRender: No protected child renders before session restoration and role/surface checks complete.

translationRule: User-facing surfaces show Arabic operational wording. Stable machine error codes remain in contracts, logs, tests, and support evidence only.
deviceCoverage: narrow and wide layouts through shared responsive UI kit
